titleInfo.title: 中国における阿彌陀三尊五十菩薩圖の圖像について──卧龍山千佛岩の作例紹介とその意義──
Chūgoku ni okeru Amida sanzon gojū bosatsu zu no zuzō ni tsuite: Garyōzan Senbutsugan no sakurei shōkai to sono igi
The Iconography of the Image of an Amitābha Triad and Fifty Bodhisattvas in China: An Introduction to Its Meaning by the Example of the Thousand-Buddha Cliff on Mount Wolong

mods.abstract: Katsuki compares in detail the following depictions of Amitabha and the 50 bohisattvas: the image (in niche 6) at Tongjiang 通江 Thousand Buddha Cliff 千佛崖, dated 663; the image on the rear wall of Wanfo Dong at Longmen, dated 680; the illustration in Mogao ku 332 (east wall, south side of the entrance) at Dunhuang, dated 698; the Wolong Shan image, dated 634. He definitely concludes that that the Wolong Shan image belongs to the same tradition as the image in cave 332 at Dunhuang (and thus to the Horyuji Golden Hall image). If there are any other conclusions, I could not understand them, as they are given in Japanese. And what the hell is the "覺禪鈔"五十二身像? = Kakuzen-shō. 136 fasc. [TZ.4.387ff, 5.1ff], compiled by Kakuzen 覺禪 (1143-?) c. 1183–1213. Shingon school 眞言宗, of which Kakuzen belonged to the Ono branch 小野流.
